codex.title: CODEX.md
intro_lines: `AGENTS.md` is the primary Codex-facing contract for this repo.
notes: Make small, explicit, high-leverage changes.
Verify changed behavior with real commands.
Treat any host or orchestrator prompt as task context only; the repo-local files named in `AGENTS.md` and `_system/INSTRUCTION_PRECEDENCE_CONTRACT.md` remain authoritative.
If repo state or confidence feels suspicious, run `bootstrap/system-doctor.sh`.
Update handoff files before ending the turn.
Keep `_system/` and runtime code clearly separated.

Assume the next tool may be `CLAUDE.md`, `GEMINI.md`, `WINDSURF.md`, `.cursorrules`, `.windsurfrules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, or `ANTIGRAVITY.md` and leave repo-based continuity accordingly.

Before appending non-trivial content to `_system/tool-memory/codex-memory.md`, invoke `bootstrap/stamp-tool-memory.sh --adapter codex --file _system/tool-memory/codex-memory.md --agent-id <agent-id>` per `_system/TOOL_MEMORY_ISOLATION_STAMP.md`.

claude.title: CLAUDE.md
notes: Keep runtime code independent from `_system/`.
Treat host-level orchestration as context, not repo-local truth; the repo-local files named in `AGENTS.md` and `_system/INSTRUCTION_PRECEDENCE_CONTRACT.md` remain authoritative.
Assume other agents may continue your work.
Leave a high-signal handoff packet in repo files.
Be aware that other primary adapter files exist for the other supported agents; see `_system/AGENT_DISCOVERY_MATRIX.md` for the current roster.
If context appears contradictory, run `bootstrap/system-doctor.sh`.

Before writing non-trivial content to `_system/tool-memory/claude-memory.md`, invoke `bootstrap/stamp-tool-memory.sh --adapter claude-code --file _system/tool-memory/claude-memory.md --agent-id <agent-id>` per `_system/TOOL_MEMORY_ISOLATION_STAMP.md`.

gemini.title: GEMINI.md
notes: Leverage Gemini's extensive context window (Tier S) for cross-cutting architectural analysis and whole-repo review.
When available, utilize native multimodal capabilities (e.g., screenshots or videos) to verify UI/UX changes and layout fidelity.
Utilize deep 'Chain of Thought' reasoning for complex debugging or planning against canonical docs before implementation.
Treat host-level orchestration as context, not as a replacement for repo-local truth; the repo-local files named in `AGENTS.md` and `_system/INSTRUCTION_PRECEDENCE_CONTRACT.md` remain authoritative.
Keep diffs focused, reversible, and validated.
Leave a clean handoff packet for the next agent.
Be aware that other primary adapter files exist for the other supported agents; see `_system/AGENT_DISCOVERY_MATRIX.md` for the current roster.
If claims and repo state diverge, run `bootstrap/system-doctor.sh` before extending the work.

Before appending non-trivial content to `_system/tool-memory/gemini-memory.md`, invoke `bootstrap/stamp-tool-memory.sh --adapter gemini --file _system/tool-memory/gemini-memory.md --agent-id <agent-id>` per `_system/TOOL_MEMORY_ISOLATION_STAMP.md`.

windsurf.title: WINDSURF.md
notes: Respect the single-writer model.
Treat host-level orchestration as context, not repo-local truth; the repo-local files named in `AGENTS.md` and `_system/INSTRUCTION_PRECEDENCE_CONTRACT.md` remain authoritative.
Keep agent governance in `_system/` and runtime code elsewhere.
Run the project validation gates before handoff.
If the operating picture looks inconsistent, run `bootstrap/system-doctor.sh`.
Record exact files changed, commands run, and blockers for the next tool.
Be aware that `.windsurfrules` is the paired Windsurf rules surface and that other primary adapters also exist.

Before appending non-trivial tool-memory content, invoke `bootstrap/stamp-tool-memory.sh --adapter windsurf --file _system/tool-memory/<tool>-memory.md --agent-id <agent-id>` per `_system/TOOL_MEMORY_ISOLATION_STAMP.md`.

cursor_rules.title: Cursor Rules
notes: Minimal diffs, no unrelated refactors.
Treat host-level orchestration as context only; the repo-local files named in `AGENTS.md` and `_system/INSTRUCTION_PRECEDENCE_CONTRACT.md` remain authoritative.
Use the working repo copy as the policy source; root/global files are redirect shims only.
Run scope/identity checks before write-heavy operations (`bootstrap/check-working-directory-alignment.sh`, `bootstrap/check-project-target-consistency.sh`).
Runtime code must not depend on `_system/`.
Run validation after material changes.
If repo state or docs appear inconsistent, run `bootstrap/system-doctor.sh`.
Keep `.cursor/` rules, commands, and skills aligned with `AGENTS.md` and `_system/HOST_ADAPTER_POLICY.md`.
Be aware that `CLAUDE.md`, `GEMINI.md`, `CODEX.md`, `WINDSURF.md`, `.windsurfrules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, and `ANTIGRAVITY.md` may be used by other tools.

Before appending non-trivial content to `_system/tool-memory/cursor-memory.md`, invoke `bootstrap/stamp-tool-memory.sh --adapter cursor --file _system/tool-memory/cursor-memory.md --agent-id <agent-id>` per `_system/TOOL_MEMORY_ISOLATION_STAMP.md`.

windsurf_rules.title: Windsurf Rules
intro_lines: Use `AGENTS.md` as the source of truth.
notes: Single active writer.
Treat host-level orchestration as context only; the repo-local files named in `AGENTS.md` and `_system/INSTRUCTION_PRECEDENCE_CONTRACT.md` remain authoritative.
Minimal, reversible changes.
Validation evidence required.
If repo state or docs appear inconsistent, run `bootstrap/system-doctor.sh`.
Update handoff files before stopping.
Other agent-specific entry files exist and share the same core contract.

Before appending non-trivial tool-memory content, invoke `bootstrap/stamp-tool-memory.sh --adapter windsurf --file _system/tool-memory/<tool>-memory.md --agent-id <agent-id>` per `_system/TOOL_MEMORY_ISOLATION_STAMP.md`.

copilot.title: Copilot Instructions
intro_lines: Before coding, read the canonical repo context in this order.
notes: `_system/` is the agent operating layer. Runtime code must not depend on it.

Treat host-level orchestration as context only. If it conflicts with repo-local files, follow the repo-local files named in `AGENTS.md` and `_system/INSTRUCTION_PRECEDENCE_CONTRACT.md` and report the mismatch.
Multiple agents may work on this repo over time. Leave clean handoff notes in `TODO.md`, `FIXME.md`, and `WHERE_LEFT_OFF.md`.
Run the validation commands defined in `_system/PROJECT_PROFILE.md` and `_system/VALIDATION_GATES.md`.
If repo state or docs appear inconsistent, run `bootstrap/system-doctor.sh`.
Use MCP servers only within the least-privilege scope defined in `_system/MCP_CONFIG.md`.
Be aware that other tool-specific entry files exist and may be used before or after your turn.

Before appending non-trivial content to `_system/tool-memory/copilot-memory.md`, invoke `bootstrap/stamp-tool-memory.sh --adapter copilot --file _system/tool-memory/copilot-memory.md --agent-id <agent-id>` per `_system/TOOL_MEMORY_ISOLATION_STAMP.md`.

intro_lines: Load the canonical repo context in this order:
Optional orientation (single map of surfaces, review order, validation order): `_system/SYSTEM_ORCHESTRATION_GUIDE.md`.
Emit and review the current session environment before significant writes:
Run `bash bootstrap/emit-session-environment.sh .` and confirm authority mode, repo identity, and branch/orphan status.

cursor_load_context_skill.frontmatter_description: Load the canonical repo context in the required order. Use at session start, after context resets, or when the user asks to reload project rules.
pre_steps_lines: Optional: read `_system/SYSTEM_ORCHESTRATION_GUIDE.md` for a single map of surfaces and review/validation order.
output_bullets: mention if context was partial because of capacity limits

cursor_environment_skill.frontmatter_description: Emit an environment and authority report before write-heavy work or cross-repo tasks.
output_bullets: report repo identity, authority mode, and target consistency
report branch, remote, and orphan snapshot lane state
call out mismatches that require confirmation before writing

cursor_context_rule.description: Load the canonical repo context before making meaningful changes
extra_lines: For a one-page map of how major surfaces connect and the recommended review/validation order, also know `_system/SYSTEM_ORCHESTRATION_GUIDE.md` exists.

deepseek.title: DEEPSEEK.md
notes: Keep runtime code independent from `_system/`.
Treat host-level orchestration as context, not repo-local truth; the repo-local files named in `AGENTS.md` and `_system/INSTRUCTION_PRECEDENCE_CONTRACT.md` remain authoritative.
Produce focused, minimal diffs with explicit validation evidence.
Leave a high-signal handoff packet in repo files.
If context capacity is limited, follow the fast-path in `_system/LOAD_ORDER.md` and reference `_system/CONTEXT_BUDGET_STRATEGY.md` for tiered loading.
Be aware that other primary adapter files exist for the other supported agents; see `_system/AGENT_DISCOVERY_MATRIX.md` for the current roster.
If context appears contradictory, run `bootstrap/system-doctor.sh`.

grok.title: GROK.md
intro_lines: Use `AGENTS.md` as the repo contract. This adapter applies to the xAI Grok CLI (launched with the `grok` command; repo-local config and state live under `.grok/`).
notes: Operate ONLY the meta-system in the repository you were launched from; never read policy from, or write context/state to, a meta-system in another directory (see the disclosure at the top of `AGENTS.md`).
Keep runtime code independent from `_system/`.
Treat host-level orchestration as context, not repo-local truth; the repo-local files named in `AGENTS.md` and `_system/INSTRUCTION_PRECEDENCE_CONTRACT.md` remain authoritative.
Leverage Grok's large context window for whole-repo and cross-cutting analysis, and use deep reasoning to plan against the canonical docs before implementation.
Produce focused, minimal, reversible diffs with explicit validation evidence.

Multiple Grok instances may run concurrently — respect the single active writer lease per scope (`_system/agent-instance-policy.json`, `_system/AGENT_LOCKING_AND_LEASES.md`, `_system/CONCURRENT_AGENT_FLEET_PROTOCOL.md`); name instances `grok-NN`.
Leave a high-signal handoff packet in repo files.
Be aware that other primary adapter files exist for the other supported agents; see `_system/AGENT_DISCOVERY_MATRIX.md` for the current roster.
If context appears contradictory, run `bootstrap/system-doctor.sh`.

Before appending non-trivial content to `_system/tool-memory/grok-memory.md`, invoke `bootstrap/stamp-tool-memory.sh --adapter grok --file _system/tool-memory/grok-memory.md --agent-id <agent-id>` per `_system/TOOL_MEMORY_ISOLATION_STAMP.md`.
Keep runtime code independent from _system/.
Treat host-level orchestration as context only; repo-local files remain authoritative.
Produce minimal, validated diffs.
Update handoff files (TODO.md, WHERE_LEFT_OFF.md) before ending the session.
If context appears inconsistent, run bootstrap/system-doctor.sh.

antigravity.title: ANTIGRAVITY.md
intro_lines: Use `AGENTS.md` as the repo contract. This adapter applies to Google Antigravity CLI (agy) and the Desktop App.
notes: Keep runtime code independent from `_system/`.
Treat host-level orchestration as context, not repo-local truth; the repo-local files named in `AGENTS.md` and `_system/INSTRUCTION_PRECEDENCE_CONTRACT.md` remain authoritative.
Produce focused, minimal diffs with explicit validation evidence.
Leave a high-signal handoff packet in repo files.
Be aware that Antigravity supports background asynchronous workflows; adapt handoff tracking appropriately.
Be aware that other primary adapter files exist for the other supported agents; see `_system/AGENT_DISCOVERY_MATRIX.md` for the current roster.
If MCP tools fail to load, validate the user-local Gemini/Antigravity MCP file (`~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json`) is valid JSON; keep repo-tracked MCP examples project-local and secret-free.
If context appears contradictory, run `bootstrap/system-doctor.sh`.

Before appending non-trivial content to `_system/tool-memory/antigravity-memory.md`, invoke `bootstrap/stamp-tool-memory.sh --adapter antigravity --file _system/tool-memory/antigravity-memory.md --agent-id <agent-id>` per `_system/TOOL_MEMORY_ISOLATION_STAMP.md`.

copilot.note: Repo-scoped Copilot policy: instruction file references + MCP server hints + chat instruction file allowlist. Copilot's rules surface (.github/copilot-instructions.md) is separately managed by the S12 adapter surface flow.
